If you are looking for information on Silybum marianum, here is a paragraph:

Silybum marianum, commonly known as Milk Thistle, is a plant species in the dandelion family with a milky sap and purple flowers. The plant has been used for centuries to promote liver health and has been found to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective properties, making it beneficial for reducing liver damage and improving liver function. The bioactive constituents of Milk Thistle include silymarin, a flavonoid complex, and silibinin, a potent antioxidant responsible for its medicinal properties. Milk Thistle is commonly prepared as a herbal supplement, tea, or tincture, often used to support liver health and protect against liver diseases.
taxonomy: Spiesia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. The genus Spiesia is native to Asia and is composed of several species of shrubs and herbs. Spiesia species are typically characterized by their small to medium-sized flowers, which are often white or yellow in color. They are also known for their trifoliate leaves, which are a common feature of many members of the Fabaceae family. Spiesia plants are often found in dry to moist woodland environments and are commonly used as ornamental plants in gardens.
